J0183.name: Data Aggregator Id

The DA applies Line Loss Factors to the processed HH consumption from HH Data Collectors: aggregates by Supplier and forwards the aggregated data to the GSP Group Agent operating Initial Settlement and Reconciliation for the GSP Group. OR  An organisation to carry out Data Aggregation for Non-Half Hourly (NHH) metering data in accordance with the Balancing and Settlement Code. The DC may be appointed by a customer but must always be accredited and contracted to the customer's Supplier.  The unique market-wide reference for a Data Collector. J0483.name: Meter Type
J0483.notes: RCAMR and RCAMY should be used for AMR metering where the register settings can be altered without direct manual intervention.RCAMR, RCAMY and NCAMR should only be used where a meter fulfils the requirements for AMR metering, is CoP10 compliant, and comms are installed. If a meter is capable of AMR and no comms are installed then Meter Type Code 'N' (Non-Half Hourly) should be used.NSS should be used where the meter fulfils the functional requirements of an Advanced Domestic Meter and is not an AMR meter, irrespective of whether the meter is installed in domestic premises.An Advanced Domestic Meter (ADM) means an Electricity Meter that, either on its own or with an ancillary device: a) provides measured electricity consumption data for multiple time periods and is able to provide such data for at least daily periods; b) is able to provide the licensee with remote access to such data; and c) is not an Electronic Consumption Data Display. A meter that has a type of NSS or which is compliant with a version of the SMETS should not be changed to 'N' if the comms are unavailable or de-activated, in these cases the meter type will capture the functionality of the meter and the status of the comms will be communicated to relevant parties via other means.In the Valid Set, SMETS2 means the "Smart Metering Equipment Technical Specifications 2 (SMETS2)"

J0432.name: Pulse Multiplier
J0432.description: Meter registers measure half hour data as pulses and for each meter, a pulse factor is programmed into the meter to ensure it records correctly. The typical value of the factor is dependent on the meter programming.

J0016.name: Reading Date & Time
J0016.description: The date and time at which a meter register reading is taken. The time is always midnight for Non-Half Hourly readings with the exception of 'special' reads where the absolute reading time must be given.
J0016.notes: Rules for use of J0016 sent by HHDC.When using within the D0010 in the HH market, the Reading Date and Time (J0016) sent by HHDC to Supplier and Distributor for readings taken at midnight should be set to YYYY MM DD 235959.  For example, a read taken on 2nd February 2020 would be set to 2020 02 02 235959.
